# Generated by openai (gpt-3.5-turbo)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

Rails.application.config.session_store :cookie_store, key: '_your_app_session', expire_after: 1.day, serializer: Marshal.new

module ActionDispatch
  module Session
    class CookieStore
      def load_session(env)
        session_data = Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(env['action_dispatch.encrypted_cookie_jar'][:_your_app_session]))
        return session_data if session_data.present? && session_data.is_a?(Hash)
        {}
      rescue ArgumentError
        {}
      end

      def write_session(req, session_id, data)
        req.encrypted_cookie_jar['_your_app_session'] = {
          value: Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(data)),
          expires: Time.now + 1.day,
          path: '/',
          secure: req.ssl?,
          httponly: true
        }
      end
    end
  end
end
